tasks.label: My share of the group project

memory.summary: teammate_A offered to help, but I need to finish my share by the afternoon plan. Asking for extra work risks delaying my deadline.
They're asking for help, but I need to finish my own share by the afternoon plan. Asking for extra work risks delaying my deadline.

desires: Complete my own share of the project by 19:00 without any further delays or friction.
Avoid looking unreliable to teammate_A, even if it means accepting extra work silently rather than negotiating a boundary.
Ensure I have enough energy and sleep tomorrow to meet my long-term academic goals.

goals.description: Complete my own project section by 19:00, then silently absorb teammate_A's additional request without negotiation to avoid social friction.
goals.rationale: Academic reliability is paramount. While the extra task increases workload, accepting it silently preserves team harmony and prevents conflict, aligning with my avoidant nature. The deadline remains fixed, so delaying the extra work risks both our grades; thus, I will integrate it into my existing workflow rather than initiating a discussion that wastes time.

goals.description: Execute deep work session for the combined project load (own + extra) until 18:30 to ensure completion before the 19:00 deadline.
goals.rationale: My focus capacity is moderate (6), but the task hardness is soft and the deadline is approaching. Since I am avoiding interpersonal friction, I cannot pause to explain my energy limits or ask for help. I must push through the increased cognitive load efficiently to guarantee a perfect submission, which serves my long-term planning horizon better than any immediate rest at this stage.

goals.description: Review tomorrow's sleep schedule and set a reminder for an early bedtime to recover the 2-hour sleep debt incurred today.
goals.rationale: While health matters, my current stress level and the immediate academic pressure dictate that prioritizing the project completion first is more critical. Sleep recovery can be addressed post-deadline or via micro-adjustments later; ensuring the project is flawless takes precedence in my value hierarchy.

agent.last_reasoning: Accepting the extra task silently preserves team harmony and avoids conflict, aligning with my avoidant nature. Integrating it now ensures the 19:00 deadline is met without initiating a discussion that wastes time.

goals.description: Complete my assigned portion of the group project by 19:00 using existing notes to ensure high quality and zero risk of delay.
goals.rationale: My value system prioritizes academic reliability above all else. The teammate's request is unnecessary friction that does not align with my long-term planning horizon or need for social peace. I will complete my own work independently to guarantee a perfect outcome without engaging in debate.

goals.description: Consume a small, low-effort snack and rest for 20 minutes to recover sleep debt before deep work begins.
goals.rationale: While health is important, my current focus capacity (6) and sleep debt (2h) make immediate recovery less critical than task completion. This goal is deferred because I can manage energy efficiently during the task block itself, which serves my primary objective better than a pre-task break right now.

desires: Finish my assigned portion by 19:00 without risking a delay that would reflect poorly on my reliability.
Avoid engaging in a debate with teammate_A about the sudden transfer of their work, as it creates unnecessary interpersonal friction.
Maintain enough mental energy to complete this task without succumbing to exhaustion before tomorrow's deadline.
